reviews.review_description:
I really wanted to like this but......I dont. Im playing on a quest 2 and it is a very stuttery, choppy experience. The gameplay is frustrating and not fun. So there is a "damage" meter.....just driving in 4x4 mode drains the meter. So this meter is basically a timer. The fact that your unable to move in 2x4 mode while towing makes this damage meter beyond frustrating and not fun. It feels like luck when I succeed as opposed to skill. 

The hand tracking with the controllers is truly awful. Yours hands get stuck in the steering wheel or they randomly release on their own. 

Game feels and plays terrible, i bought it on sale for around $5 and I feel I paid too much for it. I do not recommend even if its for free.

reviews.review_title: It’s okay, but man, choppy framerate.
reviews.review_description: Not sure if I’ll be finishing this one. The choppy framerate is an immersion killer. It’s like the frames skip when in motion, particularly when walking around. Big no no for a VR game, and overall, just a bit of a slow grind without much excitement to look past the chop.
When I downloaded it it was not loading and when I turned my vr on then off it wouldn’t let me load into my account

I can’t get enough of it! I’ve only had it for a few days, and I’m almost done with the storyline.
I’ve been playing in the realistic mode and it’s been pretty accurate! Some of the suspension mechanics are off, but other than that no complaints! I saw in some older reviews that free locomotion wasn’t an option and really that’s the only reason I hesitated on getting it, but I decided to try it anyway, and I was delighted to see that it had been added! I think it makes the game a lot more immersive. I wish there were some more vehicles, or that it would support mods so I could get them that way. Also In Power to the People the trailer with the generator is INCREDIBLY hard to get out of there, Aftermath is also a little hard to navigate. I have yet to try free play, so I may update my review once I try it. Thanks devs at Saber for making a great game!

I went from playing mud runner on console to vr and it is an amazing experience getting to actually get out of your truck and be behind the wheel of a big rig that you just drove through a river is a weird but cool experience would recommend if you like mud runner and trucks
Can't play on a couch. Half the controls are unreachable! Took a good game and made it frustrating
Great game amazing graphics but needs more vehicles and more open world to explore and some more updates
Worth the money, especially during the sales. Really fun if you love trucks and driving thru ghost towns
I like trucks
I recommend this game. The developer's have done and seem to continue to do an awesome job.

reviews.review_title: It’s come a long way since launch
reviews.review_description: Huge credit to the developers behind this game, originally bought it at launch and refunded. I bought it again to give it a second chance since I like the game on PC. This time along great experience, I love the free mode which is very immersive and “free” you get to chill in the trucks doing small tasks replayability is great! The walking I believe at launch was only teleport now you can walk around freely. I mean wow this is what it’s all about, respect to the devs who did not leave it behind and really worked on bringing this to the standards of what it should be. I recommend getting it if you like trucks and muddy mess.

This is the most funniest game Play On My Meta, Quest 2 please continue updating the game every single time. I want to relax and play a game that I don’t have to be standing. This is the right thing, ￼￼￼

reviews.review_title: FUN, HOW TO FIX LOW MEMORY
reviews.review_description:
so when i first bought this game it always gave me a low memory thing and kicked me out hand i tried loading into a game, but i think i figured out how to fix it, before turning off your oculus in the first place make sure you hold down the power button and click power off. when you turn it back on to play make sure you close out every tab on the menu, library, browser, etc. then click play on it, and if it still doesn’t work, just reboot your oculus. 

this game is so so so fun but it is a bit of a hastle to play it in the first place though
over all the game is really really good but it would be pretty good if it had multiplayer i know it's probably hard to add but this is a big company so they could probably do it

UPDATE: downgraded my review to 1 star as the game now gives an error stating low memory.  And that's using a Quest 3 (note we're talking about memory, NOT storage). Rebooted the Quest, made sure I didn't launch anything else first, MudRunner still crashes with a memory error.  I've seen another review mentioning the memory error so this is definitely an issue with the game.. Can't recommend a game you can't play. 

UPDATE 2:  Memory issue seems to have been fixed

Original Review:
This is game is an absolute blast, it has tons of replayability make sure to read the good parts of the review. But I think this game has gotten a bit too harsh criticism among the PC gamer/Snowrunner crowd. If you're familiar with the original mudrunner and Snowrunner, here are some things to note:

The bad:
-Maps are smaller due to limitations
-Winch points are dedicated points, you can't winch to just anything
-Limited number of trucks
-Gameplay is a little more linear, meaning you can't veer off the beaten path as much

The good:

-While the maps are smaller, there are a lot of them with a lot of diversity to keep it fresh.
-While the number of trucks is limited, the variety is not. You have a good number of different types of trucks, and each one has variations with different durability and fuel capacity
-The controls are exactly what you'd want in a VR game. Everything is operated in the environment in the truck. Buttons, switches, levers. All the indicators are on the dashboard to tell you fuel abdomen durability levels, and indicator lights for your AWD, diff lock, etc.
-you have the option for manual or automatic shifting right on the gear shifter
-Tasks are randomized (not sure to what extent), this keeps it fresh as your trucks will always be scattered in different states from the last task, and so you'll always have a different experience doing the tasks.
Download mud runner is not loading it's stuck on game won't load up

reviews.review_title: Its fun but mission 3—4 is glitched
reviews.review_description: Miss 3—4 ask me to pull up on the handle of the truck  and i cant
Can you add a difficulty changer because i clicked the wrong one
Overall good game runs well no issues

reviews.review_title: MudRunner VR  is Why I Escaped to PSVR2
reviews.review_description:
My Meta Quest 3 is nothing more than an expensive paperweight. The store is a digital landfill, overflowing with the VR equivalent of fast food, quick, greasy, and ultimately unsatisfying. Grid Legends? A prime example of the shovelware plague. It's like they slapped a VR sticker on a 1995's PlayStation 1 game and called it a day. I was done. Absolutely done. Then, MudRunner VR happened. This game is the ONLY reason my Quest 3 was repurposed as a doorstop. It's a brutal, unforgiving, and utterly nothingness simulation. MudRunner VR exposes the Quest 3's dirty little secret the overwhelming majority of its library is utter garbage. This game's are straight bio sludge, buried in a mountain of digital trash. I'm sick of wading through this swamp of bio. I want real VR experiences, the kind that make you forget you're wearing a headset. I want depth, complexity, and replayability like IO's psvr2's new Hitman VR upgrade That's why I'm done with the Quest 3 and on to the PSVR2. Yes, it has a wire. But it also has actual games. Horizon Call of the Mountain, Resident Evil Village VR Gran Turismo 7, no man's sky, these are the experiences I craved. These are the games that make VR worth it. But the biggest reason I've retired my Q3 is the amount of lies is beyond unfathomable GTA's San Andreas postponement indefinitely was the final nail in the coffin.

Ps. My opinion is mine & mine alone, not swayed by public opinion, shady companies, corrupt governments or paid by sponsor YouTubers. You can’t sway me, lie to me, or bribe me. I believe what I see, not what I’m told.

This game is a good game but I can’t play it because of “low memory” but I know that I don’t have low memory and it will not let me play it. I am also mad that I can’t refund the game at the end of the day I do not recommend wasting your money on this game

This game is really fun and I like the hardest mode too         But (it’s really hard) I had to do over a new campaign because I got stuck in mud and still with the biggest truck still couldn’t do it so just stick with normal difficulty until you get good at the game and good luck
Just bought the game and it won’t download. I have the space and wifi for it. Don’t know if it’s the game or not but there is $10 I want back

Not to be rude or anything but their are probably a lot of comments that say that this game is bad. If you want to ignore them, just don’t listen to what they say. But I just want to say that this game is just great for sitting people and people that like trucks. The thing I like the most is controlling vehicles like the cars and the semi’s with different tasks  to do and so much more. I hope this game gets more updates with new vehicles. Can you also maybe some time have multiplayer that is up to 1-4 players please.

I have had the game for awhile and I decided to come play but Everytime I try to load into a map it kicks me out and says I have low memory to run mud runner but I have 512gb and only 313 GB is used please help me fix this problem

Being a big snowrunner fan on the Switch and PC I was surprised to see a runner game appear on the VR market amongst the trash and slop that is out there and thinking finally something decent to pick my dust collecting Q3 up and play. What a disappointment felt like taking a step back in time to the old PS2 days maybe even b4 that. Snowrunner on my Switch is far superior, a game I may add ppl said could never run on it but looks and plays just like the PC version I know as I have both and then we come to Mudrunner!?! Q3 is capable of decent games but no another half hearted attempt and from a studio I expected better from. 

Apart from the old style gfx the controls were just as bad could have done with a option to move the on screen hud to b in a better position although it just felt half baked especially after playing powerwasher sim who had spent time on the control of the game it was a great experience and then I'm dumped with this slop oh the disappointment. I have enjoyed the Mudrunner franchise on the PC and Switch but on the Quest absolutely not and if I could refund it I would but I left it to late in hope that it would grow on me a mistake I won't be making again.

Came back to see if Sabre had done any updates or improvements but now I'm getting a message saying "Not enough memory to run Mudrunner" so things have got worse. 
Can't see Sabre fixing this oh and nothing no improvement, just going to have to throw the towel in on this game :/

I understand that the Quest 2 has limits and that's why we are lacking content... We should have DLC for the Quest 3. The base game is absolutely fantastic for an entry level experience and especially for non-mudrunner fans that are experiencing this franchise for the first time, however for us long term Mudrunner fans we crave more content than just mindless missions and a super small campaign. Add more vehicles, more maps, and maybe add something unique to specifically the VR version that would entice players to pick up a VR headset for this game. Add more things to do on your vehicle like physical maintenance. Maybe add an option to buy vehicle parts in a physical store so we to Exit the vehicle and step away from it and walk around. More stuff to do outside of the vehicle would be great to. I understand it's mudrunner, but if you're going to lack the content from the newer games at least make up for it with VR specific features.

Big fan of mudrunner expeditions on Xbox , I’m blown away how fun this is, and how satisfying running through the mud is. The graphics are better than expected, just bear in mind this is all rendered on the quest so don’t expect Xbox like graphics. 

This is certainly different than mudrunner expeditions though, it feels like a completely different game. You pretty much have to stick to the roads, rather than plot your own path. As a result, it does not really feel like mudrunner but smomething entirely. . Regardless it’s a super fun experience.

As an OG fan of the PC game, the VR version is absolutely mind-blowing. Amazing physics and guaranteed entertainment. The controls could be a bit better though. Maps seem a bit small compared to the PC version, but I understand this is due to hardware limitations. Overall, a good investment. Looking forward to SnowRunner VR!

reviews.review_title: WHAT THE OTHER REVIEWS WON'T TELL YOU!
reviews.review_description:
Mudrunner is a fun hard trucking simulation in a muddy, mostly forest environment that enables you to drive a lot of different types of trucks and perform IRL different tasks (e.g. delivering logs). I’ve completed the story mode (most Quest gamers haven’t) and I recommend the game if you like driving but trying something different and like simulating IRL actions such as turning handles in VR etc. But here’s some important facts ... 

1. There are two game modes (A) Story: which is interesting/excellent, this consist of 11 sequential chapters, each chapter has it’s own map, objectives and several vehicles to be utilised that may be different from the previous chapter(s). The story is conveyed by a single character voice-over during the chapter loading screen, and the messages you receive on your vehicle tablet from your uncle during the chapter.

2. (B.) Freeroam : you scroll through each of the different locations of story mode and select one to play. It will randomly serve up different tasks (e.g. haul two non-functioning cars back to the designated spot)

3. The environments are immersive, but the graphics are basic and have a low framerate that feels jittery (I played on Quest 3).  I recommend you don’t use the right stick to turn, as this increases the issue. Instead, play in an office chair so you can use your feet to swivel/turn left/right when walking outside your truck.

4. This is not just a driving from point a to b. A lot of the fun is all the IRL physical interactions, e.g. open the truck door with the handle, walk to the front, grab the winch, etc

5. It's a difficult game (e.g. backing a truck with little room to maneuver) and slow paced (e.g. road speed is 20 to 30 miles/hour and muddy sections 5m/h). At time of writing, the story mode Quest Achievement ‘Heading to Tow’ was unlocked in the 1st chapter by 20k gamers, but only 3k gamers unlocked the 'Halfway There'. I assume gamers swapped over to Freeroam mode to play the bite sized tasks on any map.
Worst game I ever  played my hand glitches away from me   ￼

reviews.review_title: Worth it if you catch it on sale
reviews.review_description: Man some of the people leaving reviews are typing so much bs... The main thing is you can't just sit in a regular chair or couch with arms, your right side has to be free because that's where your parking brake and shifter is in all of the vehicles and when you switch vehicles sometimes you will actually have to scoot up in your seat etc... and I wouldn't say it's a super easy game there is one mission that's almost impossible where you have to get a trailer and back it up through the woods that one will probably take you a few days to get through but other than that there's no bugs or glitches and all around pretty decent....
One of my favs! The steering took some getting use to, but now I'm good 😊. Have u guys thought about bringing SNOW RUNNER to VR as well? That would be awesome if u did! Great job devs!

Excellent controls and nice atmosphere. The graphics are not bad with good draw distances and effects. Really surprised by this one and definitely one of my favourite Quest experiences of all time. The game makes you feel like you are there perched on a side of a hill next to a  river bank edge with just your handbrake holding you there as you rush to get your winch set up.
Don’t let the other 1 stars fool you most of the 1 stars are over 5 months ago when the game came out it is worth especially on sale.There has been many updates to the game since then!

I played the whole story and now I'm playing free roam just keep hauling stuff through the mud I really enjoy the game you can actually steer and shift the trucks or you can use the toggle switch or buttons and I think it's got really good graphics
It takes 20 minutes to load and in free play small maps not enough cars.

Grinning ear to ear from the first level alone! I love Mudrunner and Snowrunner on Xbox and this takes it a whole new level! Even doing the simplest of things puts a smile on your face. You find yourself naturally leaning out of the car window to check the ground, using mirrors and turning around when you reverse etc. The attention to detail is very immersive as the sound changes when you stick your head out of the window. What an awesome game!

This is a horrible game for mechanics. Terrible traction, super slow vehicles, and it is clunky/buggy. Keep getting a black screen when i get into the warthog, loading screen pic glitches out. The steering wheel turns too slow, i have to stop to make turns. Not worth $20!  Just get the ps5 game, this is just sad in comparison.

reviews.review_title: So much bugs I cannot play this game
reviews.review_description: I tried refueling on mission 4, STILL NEEDS GAS! So many bugs, don’t buy this, waste of 20 dollars.

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to love it…
reviews.review_description:
I love mud runner/ snow runner and more recently expeditions. So getting chance to play this in VR was an exciting prospect…
However…. There are just too many little annoyances in the game, getting stuck on scenery while walking, repeatedly trying to hook the winch to objects, or open a door, or filling the tank, that awful ping ping ping noise as the engine takes damage from the diff lock, being unable to operate levers and doors without overly exaggerated gestures, makes the experience just too unforgiving to really immerse myself it, I didn’t expect awesome graphics on the Q2, they are pretty good l, but I did expect more fluid gameplay than the game offers Sorry devs, this is just not quite there in my opinion and feels like an impossible task and left me frustrated and underwhelmed by the experience. I will try and get to grips because it is now too late to return, a 2 star is all I can offer for the Q2 version, ONLY because I like the original games.If I had known before buying I would have stayed with the pancake only versions on steam.
It takes forever to load

Honestly the menus really suck and the only movement you get for your character outside of a vehicle is teleportation. Teleporting movement just gets rid of all immersive traits and the loading screens are just white text which is hard to see on the quest 2 due to the fact that white text with a black background blurs. Vehicles transmission and stuff break very unrealistically and overall the game is really unoptimized and boring.
Game is so full of bugs it's almost unplayable

i love mudrunner on pc. this is terrible and feels like a mobile game. only teleport movement, only in certain places. small details such as cat tail plants get your truck stuck and you can't walk through them. every single control is sluggish because you wait for your hand animation to finish clasping when you want to shift or turn or interact right now but can’t.

Disappointing, I played the PC game quite a bit. The first 2 hours of story mode is grueling. I know this game is supposed to be hyperrealistic and the driving isn’t meant to be fast paced by any means, but they have you driving an old junker sedan that you have to crawl at 2mph, just to complete a mission that you don’t care about because you just started the story and haven’t been given any incentives. Freemode is what you would expect, but the beginning of the story mode is disappointing

At first I had fun playing this game and then once I was getting stuck in the mud more than actually driving it started to get boring so I tried to refund it and I put I didn’t enjoy playing the game and they rejected it so now I am stuck with this game. I do not want to play.

A VR port of a known franchise that stood or still stands out with his very realistic mud physics and behavior. Or even with vehicle physics for tires and vehicle axle when driving through mud and over rough terrain.

All that above you won't get here and if there are glimps of it, you could call it Mudrunner very light (or on a budget). And of course it turned out that way because this game was release on a mobile chip so there is not enough hardware performance left to simulate all that made the Mudrunner series so iconic.

People wanted a VR version of this game, as for many non vr games. But it's always the weakest platform it gets ported too and as a result it often loses plenty of that what was loved in the non-vr version. From a marketing and financel standpoint I totally get it since the Meta Quest is the best selling HMD on the market and most users don't come from PC or console gaming (PS4, 5, Xbox X, etc.) and seem to be somewhat undemanding in the visuals.

I bought Mudrunner just to see how much is lost during the port and yes it's a bit. No real mud simulation as mentioned above, in a Mudrunner game. Yes your car will be harder to control but they shove that car asset just in the ground so it looks like its sunk into the mud. If you drive reverse and look at your path only a darker path is visible and no real marks.

Visuals are also very toned done as expected from a Quest port. Low res textures, no real shadows (only baked shadows), no real lighting, etc..

It's just tiresome to see interesting franchises getting a Meta Quest only VR port and losing many of their charm in the process. Exclusivity just plain sucks in the already small VR market.

Mud Runner VR is a 3 star game not 4 star as I had originally thought. I enjoyed the original games and I am trying to enjoy this VR adaption. MudRunner VR is a worthy buy if your a fan of the original or not. This time around it gives the viewer a VR front seat to some very rugged and muddy terrain. Many will find the controls frustrating at first but very intuitive if using the joy/thumb sticks. Once you gotten the steering figured out and under control you’ll be able to start the challenges with some fancy driving. You have some difficult objectives to complete with very limited gas and repair/damage issues are unrealistic with all vehicles. This can at times be very frustrating as you never seem to have enough of fuel or durability. Movement is slow and tedious, it  takes a very long time to go very short distances. So if your thinking you can floor it and make it go faster well, your in for a major disappointment. You’re lucky if you go faster than 10mph on the downhills. When you successfully complete a challenge you will feel relieved just to have made it. Controlling the vehicles steering wheel using the game pads sticks is the best way to control the vehicles. Only having the teleport is a glaring omission and is frustrating to use. Unable to look out the windows is just,  frankly mind-boggling! What were the developers thinking? This game will need  an update in order to get a five star. The graphics look dated and the environments all have the low res look. There is work to be done. Currently a 3 star rating is not bad. It’s not a terrible VR game, but Mudrunner VR is actually a unique experience. I do recommend buying MudRunner VR as it is. It’s a challenging game and It just needs more work and polish and a patch for the Quest 3, to update graphics because they made it playable on Quest 2 which hinders the overall gameplay. After fixing the issues then it will be a much better experience. The game  is still fun driving around in an unforgiving environment. Can’t wait until the developers add the ability to look out the side windows and improve the graphics.

I do enjoy the game and series. I do not like teleporting when on foot (would like it to be smooth). Not having multiplayer also is disappointing.  However, I do like the core gameplay of this title. The truck driving is great. Love being able to grab the wheel, shifters, and etc. I hope this will carry over to the mainline games like Snowrunner.

reviews.review_title: Do not buy Do not buy
reviews.review_description:
Played it for an hour requested a Refund and I got denied 
￼￼
My experience was disappointing at first time.  Why no tutorial?

Unfortunately, this game really makes me motion sick.   I have been using VR for at least 6 years and have paid my dues.  I have tried this on several different modes and still am experiencing motion sickness.  I hope this will be fixed in an update I’m past the refund time
The game was fun until I beat it in about 2 hours. No way to replay differently. Poor controls. No free play like advertised, just a couple more missions. No co op.

This game is great,it's missing one thing,though.  I would really appreciate the smooth locomotion option.  I've been playing VR since Quest1,and I've never had an appreciation for telaportation.  I've never had motion sickness,so I prefer the smooth locomotion setting,it's just more immersive.

I watched people play this on YouTube and it seemed like a very fun game. But it's not really you run out of gas too fast and have to restart from a checkpoint but you are just gonna run out of gas once more. Sent a refund and can't get my money back. SYAY AWAY!!!!

This game is broken for me.  There is zero sound for me in the game.  Yes, I have done all the basics and re-installed it, rebooted, looked at the menu settings.  NO SOUND!  I contacted the Devs who have ignored me.  I was reluctant to buy it anyway due to the rubbish teleport and no smooth movement on foot but I went for it anyway only to be met with this ridiculous issue.  I have owned the Quest 1, 2 and currently use the Pro and I have never experienced issues like this or a lack of response.  Seeing as the refund policy doesn't last long, I have to leave this awful review of what actually looks like a decent game.....if it actually had sound for me.

developer_response.body: Hi there, we're sorry to hear about this! Can you please reach out to our support team so they can help identify what could be causing your sound issues? https://support.saber.games/hc/en-us/requests/new

reviews.review_description: This game would be ok if I was 8 years old.  All I did was waste my money. Only for young children.!
It is a very fun and interactive off-roading game. Graphics are good.  Only problem i have with it are the glitchy/choppy load screens
Too slow and the first chapter Meat impossible
It was to relistic itake me feel mad that the game is hard
Ils Nice inserción but its weird to play and feels not Ready
I love it
I have completed story in 6 hours, nice game

Absolutely fantastic ! BUT ……. A wee update after first couple of missions…..see below this initial review for update,

This is what we always wanted in VR. As I have said in many reviews we can’t spend 24/7 deadheading hordes of zombies with a shotgun while running backwards.

A slower pace is very welcome and this looks as if it will do just that.

Only completed the first Story Mode mission , in the small little car but what a “ blast”.
So much to process , learn and remember. I can drive and it sort of does help if you have driven for real. However the controls and process takes some adjusting to.
Key, hand brake , forward and reverse but you just need to be patient. Practice and restart the game a couple of times to start to acclimatise to the unique environment.

Driving was slow at the start and hesitant for sure. Again gentle and patient initially but soon built up some speed and confidence . Even driving one handed 🤪🤣 
Was able to use mirrors and look behind so that is good.

The quality of detail in the open world around me is just a delight. I was , with some trepidation , looking around while driving and you soon start to believe you are actually there . The immersion is excellent.

Note to self ( and all Europeans ) remember this is the US so remember which side of the road to drive on 😅 I sat at a check point at a tunnel entrance on the wrong side of the barrier for ages before realising , Oopsie , other lane !! 

Felt the bumps and changes in road surfaces , excellent.
Yes , teleport not the best solution. Free movement for us VR veterans please.
One I drove out of the town my excitement elevated and was keen to start hauling cargo with big trucks. Guess I will need to practice and go carefully for a while yet.

So far , 100% delivers.  Brilliant. The garage at the start was so exciting , to actually see the Mud and Snowrunner trucks and service trailer up close. Wow!

All that remains now is to take my first truck sideways over a cliff while delivering vital bricks and spend all day figuring out how to winch / crane my self upright and to safety. What is not to love about that 💕 Can not wait 👍😊

Brilliant .

Enjoy💕.       Now the update,

Still brilliant and the truck details are superb . Really , really superb.
Driving is practice and more practice . Did steer with one hand and was able to stay in control. Used the hands on the wheel , not controllers.
Winch operation is good , odd experience having to physically do it yourself but it’s immersive.
Using the variety of gear and operation levers in trucks takes concentration and again , huge patience but feels very realistic. You do feel that being an octopus rather than human would be an advantage 🙂
Do Not Drive and attempt to engage 4WD and gear change simultaneously. You crash. Simples.
Tipping a truck back onto its wheels and towing it was huge fun , you can see out the back windows just don’t push your head too far , like beyond where the glass would be and it works fine.
Towed truck got stuck , demolished a gate and fence and then fell over dragging me with it. Now that’s Mudrunner right there. Brilliant! 

I had to get out , walk about , scratch my head and figure it out. ( and start again 🤣 )

Finally , in a jeep , I fell into a lake.  Found a tree. Winched out. 5 missions in and I was exhausted but smiling with joy .

Going to have a big lunch , get back in that cab and winch the hell out of this incredibly immersive and unique game. 

Note: I sat on an armless dining chair so I could move more freely , sofa was a nightmare. Had to shuffle my chair to match the truck seat position so that could do with being an option / setting ?

Enjoy 💕👍

As a huge fan of snow runner i went directly into this vr version of the game. Which is actually pretty good but it definitely needs some major updates.
First the fps. It's quite poor and tiring after a while. I hope for a quest 3 update to make it smoother (and ideally sharper).
Second is the lack of out of the window view. Why?? This was supposed to be the most important aspects of vr, to have best possible peripheral vision. And it misses!
And third, the lack of free movement. Not as important as the others but since you are doing everything 1 to 1(turn keys, open doors, filling gas etc) why not moving like normal?
As i said i really like the game, i like the missions and i don't mind being smaller than flat versions. But for now it's hard for me to pay it so I'm gonna leave it aside until you can fix frames and free view. Thank you
Perfect game for relaxing and driving
Get er done

reviews.review_title: Game ok but few annoying things
reviews.review_description:
1. Teleport only
2. Stucking in mud to tedious. Even trying to go in the small car from Soviet era around the mud on a harder surface like grass is causing the car behavior like stucking in a mud.
3. I could not gain higher speed than 20km/hr in the small car even on asphalt. Why?
4. To hold the steering wheel requires holding the grab button depressed which is painful. Should be sticking to the wheel after „clicking” this button.

Other than that, this game has potential and is promising a good fun. Hopefully, the devs will listen and fix these issues in future updates. The game is worth the $20 .
SMOOTH LOCOMOTION FOR ALL LOL  also this game has me not wanting to live regular life I want to eat sleep play mudrunner now... I need my life back 🤣

Really wish Sabre would have made this more like Snowrunner or the OG Mudrunner with ability to purchase a shop and other trucks and upgrade vehicles. The teleport movement gets pretty annoying sometimes. The steering option is trash but overall a great game I’ve spent so many hours playing already. Can’t wait to see what kind of patches and upgrades Sabre has in mind for this already sweet game!
This game is so good! Def my new fav game!
Love this game
Brilliant game! Mud! Hills! Swamps! Getting stuck , running out of petrol! Great fun… Just let me wind down the window and look out behind when I’m reversing

reviews.review_title: Please, please add a Polish language.
reviews.review_description:
Very good game. Very immersive. 
Please add Polish language texts.
Please add a full locomotion.
Game sometimes have micro lags during drive and the look on the left or right. This is not a big problem. Maybe future optimisations fix it.

Super game. I recommend this game to drive simulator fans.

Really fun game. Nothing like it on the quest store. For me personally I think this is one of the best games I've played on the quest 3 so far. Well done Devs for making this vr 👍 I'd love to see American truck SIM ets2 make it to the quest

Great game worth every penny for the immersive experience like no other with great thinking involved it's not a fast game it's all about taking your time plotting your next move fantastic game series as a whole
Originally I was going one star it but they changed the support

I played the first 4 missions and i'm having so much fun with this game. It works great. It looks great and it sound great. 
It has some flaws.

1. Please give us free walking instead of teleport.
2. I wish for an option that the music continues to play even when you're outside the car. The whole vibe changes outside. I like the music.
3. Sometimes the steering is acting strange. But this could be the tracking of the motion controls.
4. Would like an option to disable highlights and the areas marked around your vehicle. For more immersion.
5. Option to adjust height of the player.
6. Ive played Snowrunner and if i remember correct in that game you can attach your hinch to all possible things. In MudRunner vr there are only certain points to atrach and its almost a spoiler seeing all those points on the trees
7. Would be nice if you can drive a little faster on the normal roads. 

Overall its a great game and having fun with it. Thanks for making this saber
